---
title: "Choo Liu"
image: "docs/me.png"
links:
  - label: Github
    url: "https://github.com/chooliu/"
  - label: Google Scholar
    url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=jhxd_lYAAAAJ"

output:
  postcards::solana
---
 
### About Me

Hi! My name is Choo, a PhD Bioinformatics student at UCLA.

My career began as a molecular biologist in biotech (e.g., 10X Genomics), followed by statistical training (MS Biostatistics; Colorado School of Public Health / Anschutz Medical Campus statistics consulting core).

These past lives have converged in my work in the Luo Lab at UCLA (PI: Chongyuan Luo), where our group develops multiomic single-cell and spatial assays. My dissertation work extends statistical genetics to these new tools, particularly focusing on how genetic variation and 5mC-methylation interact with aging and neuropsychiatric phenotypes.

---

Learning about other folks' research and teaching (including undergraduate student supervision) are my favorite parts of being in academia.

Please reach out if you'd like to collaborate or grab a coffee.

---


#### **Recent Highlights**

Eyring & Liu, et al. (2026) *A Single-Cell Atlas of DNA Methylation in Autism Spectrum Disorder Reveals Distinct Regulatory and Aging Signatures in Prefrontal Cortex.* [bioRxiv](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.06.17.660162v1)

Li, et al. (2025) *Integration of Polygenic Risk with Single Cell Methylation Profiles for Depression.* [bioRxiv](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2025.03.11.642615v1)

Krockenberger, et al. (2025) *FastGxC: Fast and Powerful Context-Specific eQTL Mapping in Bulk and Single-Cell Data.* [bioRxiv](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2021.06.17.448889v2)

---


#### Other Interests

clinical volunteering (mental health and dementia settings), botany, synthesizers, field recordings, internet history/doomscrolling, rain and rocky beaches (pictured above: Lands End), film noir, and immediately misplacing all of my pencils and pens.
